rulings.Case Title: ANA MACDONALD VS. DUKE PARTNERS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Dismiss Action, Or In The Alternative, To Enforce Settlement Agreement; And To Award Attorneys Fees In The Amount Of $2,422.50
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 26, 2022, line 1 DEFENDANT DUKE PARTNERS, LLC'S Motion To Dismiss Action, Or In The Alternative, To Enforce Settlement Agreement; And To Award Attorneys Fees In The Amount Of $2,422.50 1. Defendant's Motion to Dismiss Action to Enforce Settlement Agreement is moot. Moving party has already obtained a judgment for possession and the Court has already scheduled on OSC re: dismissal. See August 17, 2021 order: "Pursuant to California Rules of Court, Rule 3.1385 the court will dismiss this case unless good cause is shown as to why it should not be dismissed." The issue of dismissal after reported settlement is currently pending in department 610 and shall be addressed there as calendared. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: GARRITT BLANZ ET AL VS. ROBERT C HINCKLEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Terms Of Stipulation For Settlement Under Ccp Section 664.6 [Per Email Dated 12/7/2021]
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 26, 2022, line 2, Plaintiff'S Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Terms Of Stipulation For Settlement Under Ccp Section 664.6 [Per Email Dated 12/7/2021] 1. Plaintiff's Motion for Entry of Judgment per Stipulation. Hearing required. No later than 4:00 p.m. on January 25, 2022 counsel for the parties to meet and confer and prepare for the Court drafts of (a) a release and settlement agreement; (b) an easement to be recorded and (c) an access agreement which would allow the parties temporary access as reasonably needed for maintenance and repairs. All provisions of the drafts to which parties both agree shall be in black; all contested provisions shall contain two versions - red version representing Blanz' proposal and blue version representing Hinkley's proposal. The final draft shall be e-mailed to the Court no later than 8:00 a.m. on January 26, 2022. No later than 8:00 a.m. on January 26, 2022 counsel for both sides to appear in-person and engage in meaningful meet and confer efforts regarding the disputed provisions. Counsel to sign a sign-in sheet on the Department's door indicating the time of the arrival. The case will be called at 11:00 a.m. to address the parties' progress and the motions on the merits. The Court will address the enforceability of the oral settlement agreement entered on the record and the good faith of the parties and their counsel representing to the Court that the matter was resolved on the material terms recited. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: VERONICA PEREZ VS. JACQUELINE RODRIGUEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 26, 2022, line 3 CROSS DEFENDANT CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Cross-Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment -is GRANTED. The government claims filed by Defendant/Cross-Complainant in 2019 are inadequate to support the cross-complaint for indemnity. The time period in which Defendant/Cross-Complainant could have filed a claim has expired. Therefore Defendant/Cross-Complainant did not exhaust their administrative remedies and the cross-complaint is dismissed with prejudice. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: CHINA LIVE VENTURES LIMITED, L.P. VS. 644 BROADWAY LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 26, 2022, line 4, DEFENDANT 644 INVESTORS, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT 1. Defendant's Demurrer to Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to the fourth, fifth, and sixth causes of action. Plaintiff failed to amend the original complaint to allege additional facts to support each element of the causes of action as applicable to 644 Investors. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: CHINA LIVE VENTURES LIMITED, L.P. VS. 644 BROADWAY LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 26, 2022, line 4, CROSS DEFENDANT GEORGE CHEN'S DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT 2. Cross-Defendant's Demurrer to Amended Cross-Complaint is SUSTAINED with leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action for quiet title for Cross-Complainant to allege facts that cross-defendant holds a personal interest in the property; and OVERRULED as to the eighth and ninth causes of action. Cross-Complainant alleged facts sufficient to support a cause of action for Intentional Interference with Prospective Economic Advantage by Cross-Defendant as an agent or in the alternative in Cross-Defendant's individual capacity. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: MARIE GRACE JENNIFER PASILABAN WONG, TRUSTEE VS. JESUS CHAVEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 26, 2022, line 6, DEFENDANT JESUS CHAVEZ'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT Defendant's Demurrer to Amended Complaint is overruled, 5 days to answer. Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer. Factual disputes as to the meaning of the lease term provisions are not appropriate on a demurrer. Complaint is not uncertain within the meaning of CCP 430.10(f). Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
